storyInfo: While making her way back to the Tianshi Bureau for study, Lin Qingyan receives a letter from Zuo Le, asking her to investigate Yun Qingping's disappearance. Doing so, however, gets her sucked into a strange little town, and a different, utterly baffling case involving the theft of a secret tome of martial arts.

attributes.content: Our tale begins in a modest teahouse, its building hardly tall enough for the two floors it's split into.

The interior is simply furnished, the space neither cramped nor bare. Ten wooden tables, meticulously laid out, seat six each. The tea stains have been scrubbed as much as they'll permit. It all speaks to a diligent owner.
The teahouse's server casts ladles of water across the floor, as he mops each and every tile to a mirror sheen.
It seems a while since this teahouse last welcomed a guest. One wonders what sort of customer could warrant such a conscientious welcome.
And with perfect timing, they come in.
One, two... A party of two? Welcome, this way. Would you like a pot of tea to start?
attributes.name: Teahouse Server

attributes.content: We're fine. First things first—is this Mangshan Town?
attributes.name: Menacing Man

attributes.content: It is Mangshan Town.
attributes.name: Teahouse Server

attributes.content: Are you the shopkeep of this place, or the server?
attributes.name: Menacing Man

attributes.content: I am the server.
attributes.name: Teahouse Server

attributes.content: Keep not in?
attributes.name: Menacing Man

attributes.content: The keep is not in.
attributes.name: Teahouse Server

attributes.content: It's like I'm hearing myself echo. At least you give answers.
attributes.name: Menacing Man

attributes.content: One more question then—did a stranger from outside come by today?
attributes.name: Menacing Man

attributes.content: They did.
attributes.name: Teahouse Server

attributes.content: When? What did they look like?
attributes.name: Menacing Man

attributes.content: Right now. Two people, both bandit-like.
attributes.name: Teahouse Server

attributes.content: Wang ba dan, don't you screw around with us!
attributes.name: Boorish Man

attributes.content: The boor of a man's temper flares, and he throws a hefty palm across the server's face that sends him crashing to the floor.
But the server does not anger in kind. He calmly picks himself up, and stands up again, like none of that just happened.
What strange guests you are. No tea, no wine? It's beyond me to serve you, then.
attributes.name: Teahouse Server

attributes.content: Kid, be nice. He just works here.
attributes.name: Menacing Man

attributes.content: Check the kitchen in the back. And close every door shut while you're in there.
attributes.name: Menacing Man

attributes.content: Take it easy. Of course we want tea.
attributes.name: Menacing Man

attributes.content: But we're waiting for someone first. We need to have our tea with her.
attributes.name: Menacing Man

attributes.content: And in short order, the proclaimed plus-one arrives.
Scarcely has the girl stepped inside to look around when the tip of a crossbow bolt nestles against her back, and the point of a machete to her throat.
Haha...
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: Look at that, it's the Lu brothers. Who knew I'd bump into you here?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: I think you wouldn't have come if you did know.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Still, good to see you. Now stick around.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Server boy. Shut the door, if you don't mind. And hang up the closed sign.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Then, we'll take a pot of tea.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Here's your tea. Take your time.
attributes.name: Teahouse Server

attributes.content: The 'Three Blades of Wind and Cloud' treating me to tea? All the heartlands know you. I feel like a VIP now.
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: Shame it's just the two of us now.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: A few days ago, you framed our trio with that secret tome. That sicced three whole gangs and the Yumen garrison on our asses. A hundred men, maybe more, all hunting us down.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: I made it, he made it. Our youngest brother died outside Baizao.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: So... what could we do but invite you for a cup of tea?
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Haha... Absolutely, we should say cheers to you jianghu heroes for making it. Here, may I toast you two with my tea, in place of wine? And then, after meeting on such good terms, we can part on them too?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: You need to shut the—!
attributes.name: Lu the Middle

attributes.content: Let's cut the chit-chat—do you still have the secret tome?
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: So you caught wind of the deal? Is that why you were waiting for me here?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: That's right.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: And what happened to my guy, Mr. Pu? Don't tell me you killed him?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: Guess.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: *sigh*... And there's nothing I can say to convince you that you don't want this tome?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: You already screwed us over once. If we don't get this tome today, then the wrong you stuck on us, and the death of our third brother, will both go unanswered.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Alright, now I'm curious. Do you fine heroes really know how this tome here came to be?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: Of course.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Rumor has it the Grandmaster of Yumen, who perfected the martial arts, wrote all his learnings in a tome before wading out of the jianghu.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: And not long ago, that tome finally fell into the commoners' hands, though almost no one can actually say they've seen it.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: We brothers did some asking around too. All signs pointed to you having the tome.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: So presumably, you two want the tome so you can master the ultimate kung fu? Claim First Under Heaven?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: What I'm asking is, what will this do for you in the long run? Will you rule the jianghu with an iron fist? Will this avenge your dead brother?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: Or are you going to duel to the death once you realize there can only be one First Under Heaven?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: You're pushing your luck—bring it out.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: He says, making a subtle show of his machete again.
To which the girl can only shake her head and sigh. She pulls out a small parcel from her travel bag. Its rectangular dimensions are those of a book, no doubt.
Fine, fine. It's not like I needed the tome anyway, I was just gonna sell it to—well, anyone.
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: So, fine gentlemen, what's your bid? Because as a note, the last buyer I had lined up set the bar high.
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: She says, gesturing a '7' in the air.
I have a better offer.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Your life.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: *sigh*... Sir, there's no reasoning with you, if you do business like this.
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: As I see it, it's already a very fair offer.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: If my info's right, the Grandmaster's tome was meant for the general leading the Yumen guards.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: I don't know how you got your hands on it, but it can't have been legal.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: We're bandits, fine lady, and you're a crook. We do us and you do you. Justice and injustice are both above our paygrade.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Whoof, you're no fun... Alright, alright, I really screwed the hound on this one this time. You've got the stuff, can I go now?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: Not yet.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: I may not be a delicate man, but I know a trouble magnet when I see it. If I let you blab about this to the entire jianghu, I fear we won't live to train as the tome instructs.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Well, if you can cram it a little, and get a little smarter too... Who knows?
attributes.name: Clever Girl

attributes.content: Save some jibber-jabber for your next life.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: And if there is a next life, remember this: Play stupid games, win stupid prizes.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: What the...?
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: The man can hardly process the sight of his machete dropping to the ground.
His unwavering grip has never betrayed him throughout his grueling years in the jianghu.
Yet now he can't even hold his own blade.
*pant*... *pant*... Sh... shit... Bro... I think...
attributes.name: Lu the Middle

attributes.content: Someone... here... poisoned us...!
attributes.name: Lu the Middle

attributes.content: I poisoned you.
attributes.name: Teahouse Server

attributes.content: Producing a hemp rope from out of nowhere, the server silently busies himself. In a flash, the three incapacitated guests are bound up like zongzi on the plate, and deposited neatly in the corner.
Figures I'd mistake you for just a teahouse server, sir. My eyes must be going bad.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Poisoning us so finely, you must be a jianghu old-timer too? Tell us your name. If we're dying, at least we should go out knowing.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: You weren't mistaken, I'm just a teahouse server, no jianghu old-timer. My friends call me 'Hu Jun'.
attributes.name: Teahouse Server

attributes.content: I imagine you know Mr. Pu too, then?
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: He's one of those very friends.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Heh...
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: One more thing, if you don't mind. This tome... why do you want it?
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: To leave this town.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: It holds five hundred and twenty families, and going around visiting each one takes two thousand and nine hundred steps.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: For years upon years, neither number has changed at all. I want to get away from here.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: But if I want to leave, there needs to be some change. And if I want some change, I need this secret tome.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: I don't understand what you're getting at, sir...
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: No matter. It's not as if all the realm was made for human minds to comprehend.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Yeah, fair.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: We've fallen to you, I admit it! Kill us or whatever, just do what you like.
attributes.name: Lu the Older

attributes.content: Whether or not you admit you've fallen to me doesn't change anything.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Sir, you've gotten what you came for. Why bother taking anyone's life?
attributes.name: Bookish Teen

attributes.content: Who the hell are you? When did you seat yourself?
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: If you'd have my name, it's Yun Qingping. I was just passing through on my travels.
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: I've been sitting here since noon, actually. You were just so fixated on the secret tome, I naturally didn't enter your sight.
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: Catching sight of the elusive boy, Hu Jun grips his dagger tight, but following a moment of thought, he lets go again.
I have no reason to fear you. I 'treated' all the water in this teahouse. You should be poisoned too.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: You wouldn't need to fear me anyway, sir. I'm just a scholar who couldn't even truss a featherfowl. There's nothing I could do to you in a fight.
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: But you insist on saving these people?
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Yes. I ask for you to look down on them with kindness, and not to kill again.
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: These people are not good people.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: If he'd gotten the tome just now, he would've silenced everyone else all the same—he even said so himself, just now.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: They may be utter scoundrels, but surely your heart is still a good one, sir. So why sully your own innocence for them?
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: Just hand them to the authorities, and justice will take care of them as a matter of course. As for the secret tome, I'll swear to keep my mouth shut.
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: How about it, sir?
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: The way you talk reminds me of my friend. He's a good person. You must be too.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: But for now, I cannot trust you.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: I won't kill you, but I will ask you to step forth, so I can tie you up.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: *sigh*...
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: You didn't want trouble, and yet trouble's come knocking.
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: No noise. Or else all four of you are dead.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: (Cooperative nod)
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: Excuse me, is this teahouse closed for the day?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.text: \n\nI hope this letter finds you well.
\n\nI heard about your resignation. I don't know of the specifics, but I admire you deeply for your courageous act. No wonder Mother always exhorts me to follow your example.
\n\nTo make a long story short, I have something urgent I want to entrust to you in this letter.
\n\nAn old friend of mine, called Yun Qingping, came with me to Baizao. We've been due to return and report back to the Sui Regulator. But three days ago, he went missing.
My friend is a very careful person. Going AWOL like this is unusual to say the least. I fear for the worst.
\n\nBased on the clues I've found, he was last headed out of the city via the north edge, in the direction of Mt. Mang, same as your return trip to the Tianshi Bureau.
I was going to search for him myself, but the Sui Regulator has been inundated lately, not to mention whispers of another incident in Father's army. I'm all but stuck here.
\n\nYou're the only one who can help me find my friend's whereabouts now, Auntie. I've enclosed a photo of him with this letter. If you find him, I beg you contact me ASAP.
\n\nMay your health be sound, and your vacation merry.
\n\nP.S. Please show up at our next reunion dinner, Auntie. I'm sure Father and Mother are eager for the story surrounding your resignation.

attributes.content: That morning...
The nerve of this boy. Now he's sending me on missions...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: ...'Courageous act', what nonsense...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: No respect for elders...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Before she knows it, Lin Qingyan has set foot into this strange town.
It's a rustic one, low buildings littered about. Few wander the streets. The scene here is a still and peaceful one.
Did Mt. Mang always have a town like this at its foot?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: This is odd. I lost signal as soon as I entered. I didn't think the Tianshi Bureau had missed any nearby towns with its signal towers...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Young lady, young lady—
Hm?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Young lady—I've never seen you before. Are you from outside? How rare, how very rare.
attributes.name: Grinning Passerby

attributes.content: Correct... Are outside visitors infrequent here?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: They used to be. Lately there's been more.
attributes.name: Grinning Passerby

attributes.content: I'm not surprised. The entrance to this town is very remote, and not marked on any maps to boot...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Sorry, do you happen to know the way to the nearest Tianshi Bureau?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Tianshi Bureau, Tianshi Bureau—can't say I've heard, can't say I've heard.
attributes.name: Grinning Passerby

attributes.content: Can you point me to the road out of town, then? I didn't take full note when entering, and I lost the way I came by the time I thought to look back.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Out of town, out of town—I don't know, I've never been out.
attributes.name: Grinning Passerby

attributes.content: You don't know?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Slow down, slow down—have a pancake, take your time, look around. Everything is done when it's done. No rushing it.
attributes.name: Grinning Passerby

attributes.content: I'm assuming you live here? How long have you lived here for?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Me, me—yes I do. I've been here ever since a pastry lit the first fire in my belly.
attributes.name: Grinning Passerby

attributes.content: Lit a fire? In your belly...?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: *ahem*... Thank you, I'll keep looking.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Young lady, young lady—pancakes, fresh from the oven. Have one.
attributes.name: Grinning Passerby

attributes.content: No thanks, I'm not hungry... Did you just dig that out from your pocket? That pancake is oven-fresh?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: It's done now, well-done now.
attributes.name: Grinning Passerby

attributes.content: Ouch! This is scalding! You were holding this to your chest—?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: No rushing it, no rushing it. Go back to looking, young lady, until you're heated through just right.
attributes.name: Grinning Passerby

attributes.content: Excuse me, wait!
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Eight thousand nine hundred and ninety-one. Eight thousand nine hundred and ninety-two. Eight-thousand...
Ah.
Careful! Are you hurt?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Gaze fixed on the ground, a young woman walks headfirst into Lin Qingyan and promptly tumbles straight to the ground, where something falls out of her clothes.
Lin Qingyan immediately checks her over, then turns to look back, only to find the old man with the pancakes is nowhere to be seen.
Umbrella, my umbrella... Mister told me not to let go of my umbrella...
Here. And this letter. It fell out of your sleeve.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: The girl hurriedly takes her umbrella, then with difficulty stuffs the letter back in her sleeve.
Lin Qingyan looks to the clear blue sky.
Try and stand up, young miss. Let me help you.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: (This girl's so light...)
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: How far did I count just now?
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Er, eight thousand nine hundred and ninety-three?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Eight thousand nine hundred and ninety-four. Eight thousand nine hundred and ninety-five...
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Young miss, are you alright? Do you need me to take you home?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Home? I can't go home, I promised to meet him at the tofu stall—
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Ah... oh no, he told me it was a secret, to protect it with my life, not to tell anyone even if they beat me to death!
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Did you hear that? Did you hear about my secret meeting with him at the tofu stall?
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: That's your personal business. Let's say I didn't hear.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Thank goodness, I'd rather not get beaten to death—where did I count up to?
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Eight thousand nine hundred and ninety six? What are you counting?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: He told me to count to nine thousand when I go to meet him, and once I do—oh no, I said it again! I'm going to get beaten to death!
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: If I'm not missing something, I think 'beat you to death' is just a brand of Yanese hyperbole...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Unless you're telling me, Miss, you believe your life is in danger—
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Eight thousand nine hundred and ninety-seven, eight thousand nine hundred and ninety eight...
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Er...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Eight thousand nine hundred and ninety-nine, nine thousand! It's time! I'm off to see him—
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: There's... something strikingly odd about the people in this town...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: ...What's this? Is this... It's the letter that fell out of her sleeve.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: 'CLASSIFIED SCOOP, NOT FOR PRYING EYES'?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Wait, Miss!
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Hu Jun, Hu Jun—I'm here to see you!
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Shh, quiet. This way.
Hu Jun... Why did I have to count to nine thousand before seeing you? My lips are chapped from counting out loud now.
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: I had no other choice. That's the only way you know to keep track of time.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Did anyone see you come here?
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: A bunch of people, but none of them know I was coming to see you!
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: You get the stuff?
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Yep, in Mister's cabinet, second drawer. It said 'CLASSIFIED SCOOP, NOT FOR PRYING EYES' on it.
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Perfect. Give it to me.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: A-Alright...
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Oh no, I can't... I can't find the letter! I must have lost it on the way!
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: But that's alright, I read what was inside. It said 'Three quarters past the Hour of Wei, deal at the old teahouse'.
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: You sneaked a look at the contents, and you lost the letter itself.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: What's wrong, Hu Jun? You're crinkling your forehead up so hard. It makes you look terrifying. I don't like it.
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: You're sure that's what it said? The time? Three quarters past the Hour of Wei?
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Yeah! I can't actually tell the time, but my memory's really good.
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Hu Jun, what's wrong? Say something. Do I have to count to nine thousand again before you stop ignoring me? Okay, one, two, three, four, five...
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: No, stop that.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Hu Jun, Hu Jun—I really don't like you being this way. Your face looks so scary.
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Xiaoshu, I trust you dearly. If everything goes smoothly, I was planning to get out of here with you. We could leave together.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: But you're not ready yet. You haven't learned to keep secrets. That's why I can't—
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: What are you doing?
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: You can't hide it from me. Your eyes are wet, Hu Jun. You're hurting a lot, aren't you?
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Mister says, when things get wet, I shouldn't jump straight to wiping them, but I can help by blowing on them. Are your eyes a little drier now?
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Ah, you held my hand too tight just now! Now there's wrinkles in it.
attributes.name: Umbrella Girl

attributes.content: Goodbye, Xiaoshu.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: This is the direction she went, I know it. How did she lose me so fast?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: She said she was meeting up at the tofu stall. That should be right—ah, there.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Far across the alley, Lin Qingyan spots the umbrella the girl was sporting.
She hurries over, intending to return the lost letter, but as she draws near it becomes obvious—
The umbrella lies forlorn on the ground, the girl unaccounted for.
Welcome, this way. Sit wherever you like.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: I ask because I saw the sign hung up outside. Are you closed or not?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: If a guest comes for tea, then we can hardly keep closed.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Would you like a pot of tea to start?
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Duoxie.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: The scent of the green tea is positively pungent. Lin Qingyan reaches a hand, but stops.
You're the server of this teahouse? Do you have time for a few questions?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Please, ask away. I'll do all I can to answer.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: I see this teahouse is situated in the most bustling part of town. Why close for the evening in the middle of the afternoon?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: We play it by ear, you see. Sometimes, nobody wants a drink of tea.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: But what if a customer was coming here for something other than tea?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Sometimes, our guests need a shelter from the rain and a spell to rest. They're welcome inside to sit for a while, yes.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Drink tea, or don't. But if you don't take tea and you don't plan on leaving quietly, then the problems begin.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: That's fair enough... You make me wonder. Did you get any troublesome customers like that today, before closing up shop?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: My guests today all drank tea.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: Last question...
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: It just caught my eye, honestly, but there's what looks like a spot of paint stuck to your lapel.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: You're a teahouse waiter, not a studio painter. Where would you get paint like that on your clothes from?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: And one more thing. I happened to see this color of paint today, on an umbrella.
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: You look awfully anxious. What's the matter?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: I mean, look at the time... Didn't you close it ages ago? Your deal, I mean, three quarters past the Hour of Wei?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Brain working in overtime, Hu Jun promptly flips the table up, cutting off Lin Qingyan's line of sight to him. It's just long enough for him to scramble like mad to the upper floor.
Halt—!
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: H... help...
attributes.name: Feeble Call

attributes.content: You.
attributes.name: Hu Jun

attributes.content: You have the secret tome... So he tricked me...
attributes.name: Heartless Swordswoman

attributes.content: Give it to me.
attributes.name: Heartless Swordswoman

attributes.content: You asked for it, then.
attributes.name: Heartless Swordswoman

attributes.content: Thank you, Deputy Minister Lin... I never imagined I'd run into you here.
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: How do you know me—wait, hold on, you're... Yun Qingping?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: I've heard much about you from Zuo. It's an honor to finally meet—
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: But according to Zuo Le, you're missing?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Missing? Did Zuo not see the letter I left him? I really feel like I should've foreseen that...
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: Set that aside for now. What's going on here?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: The three beside me got into a fight, I think, over some kind of treasure, and then—
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: Three? What do you mean three?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Lin Qingyan glances over again. There's two bandits bound up like fowl, but all that lies next to them is a pile of severed hemp rope.
Well, that's strange...
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: Curses—!
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Lin Qingyan hurries out into the street, where a good few townsfolk are milling around to see what the commotion is.
She hears the single sharp strike. She sees someone crash through the upper floor window and fall to the road hard, right in front of the teahouse's entranceway.
She fully expects to see someone injured. Possibly even a corpse. But all that greets her eyes...
Is a pile of shattered teapot shards.
